ontology.foodon_gap_note: No exact FoodOn entry for Chinese cabbage and minced pork soup. Closest defensible anchor is pork soup food product (FOODON_03311737). Cabbage component not individually coded.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: No single FoodEx2 code covers the composite dish. A0FJP (minced pork) is used as the primary protein anchor. Chinese cabbage (napa cabbage) component is not separately coded here.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code identified for this composite Vietnamese/Singaporean soup dish.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard bowl of Chinese cabbage and minced pork soup is approximately 250 g, representing a typical single-serve portion as consumed in a Singaporean or Vietnamese home meal.

Per scoring model guidance for negligible-carbohydrate foods, glycemic_index is set to the numeric sentinel value 0 with gi_category 'not_applicable' to satisfy the requirement that glycemic_index must be numeric. Glycemic load per serving is 0.0 by calculation (GL = GI × carbohydrate_g_per_serving / 100; carbohydrate = 0 g).

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium 1050 mg/100g — far exceeds the 600 mg/100g upper threshold
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should avoid or strictly limit this dish as the sodium content of 1050 mg/100g is nearly double the recommended upper limit; if consumed, reduce fish sauce and salt in the broth substantially and increase the cabbage-to-broth ratio to partially offset sodium with natural potassium.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Zero carbohydrate and zero sugar; high protein 19.1 g/100g supports glycemic stability
type2_diabetes.guidance: This dish is suitable for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes as it contains 0 g carbohydrate and 0 g sugar per 100 g, and the high protein content from minced pork supports satiety and glycemic stability; monitor total meal carbohydrate from accompanying rice or other side dishes.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Sodium 1050 mg/100g and estimated saturated fat from minced pork elevate cardiovascular risk
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should limit this dish due to its very high sodium content (1050 mg/100g) and the saturated fat contribution from minced pork; if consumed, use lean pork mince, reduce fish sauce and salt, and serve in small portions alongside high-potassium vegetables.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: High sodium worsens tissue swelling; pork-derived arachidonic acid amplifies inflammatory pathways
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Individuals with rheumatoid arthritis should limit this dish as the sodium load of 1050 mg/100g exacerbates joint inflammation and the arachidonic acid (omega-6) from minced pork amplifies inflammatory pathways; choose leaner pork mince, reduce fish sauce and salt, and increase the proportion of Chinese cabbage to benefit from its sulforaphane anti-inflammatory content.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Canh Cải Thảo Thịt Băm (Chinese Cabbage and Minced Pork Soup) is a light Vietnamese-style clear broth soup widely consumed in Singapore and Vietnam. It is prepared by simmering seasoned minced pork balls or loose minced pork with napa cabbage (Chinese cabbage / cải thảo) in a broth seasoned with fish sauce, salt, garlic, and shallots. The soup has a mild, savoury flavour with a slightly sweet note from the cabbage, and a tender texture from both the softened cabbage leaves and the cooked pork mince. It is a common home-cooked dish served as part of a shared Vietnamese or Vietnamese-Singaporean meal alongside steamed rice and other side dishes. Nutritionally, the dish is high in protein (19.1 g/100g) and moderate in fat (10.5 g/100g) with negligible carbohydrate (0 g/100g), making it glycemically neutral. However, the sodium content is very high at 1050 mg/100g due to fish sauce and salt in the broth, which is a significant concern for individuals with hypertension, cardiovascular disease, or rheumatoid arthritis. The dish provides modest amounts of calcium (19 mg/100g), iron (1.14 mg/100g), and magnesium (6 mg/100g). It is gluten-free and dairy-free but is not suitable for halal, vegetarian, or vegan diets due to the pork content.

confidence_reason: Core macronutrient and micronutrient values are sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database (cr_id G00010549, confidence 0.95 per source record). Saturated fat is estimated from USDA FoodData Central pork mince data and documented accordingly. All other fields are constructed from defensible analogous sources or are not applicable.
notes: carbohydrate_g is confirmed zero per HPB Singapore Foodo database (G00010549); this is a protein-and-fat-dominant soup with no starchy ingredients. sugar_g is confirmed zero per HPB source. dietary_fibre_g is reported as 0.0 by HPB; this is unexpected given the Chinese cabbage content but is accepted as reported from the canonical source — the value likely reflects the broth-dominant composition of the dish as measured. saturated_fat_g (3.6 g/100g) is estimated from USDA FoodData Central entry for ground pork (NDB No. 10219, approximately 20% fat pork mince) proportioned to the fat content reported by HPB; this is an approximation and not directly reported by HPB. monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g set to -1: detailed fatty acid profile not reported by HPB for this dish entry and not available from a directly matching approved source as of 2026-04-16. vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, water_g set to -1: these micronutrients are not reported by HPB for this dish entry; detailed vitamin and mineral panel not available in HPB Foodo or USDA FoodData Central for this specific composite dish as of 2026-04-16. Glycemic index: this food contains negligible carbohydrates (0.0 g/100g per HPB); gi_category is set to not_applicable. glycemic_index is set to the numeric value 0 as a sentinel to satisfy the scoring model requirement that glycemic_index must be numeric; this does not imply a measured GI of zero but reflects the not-applicable status for a negligible-carbohydrate food. glycemic_load_per_serving is 0.0 by calculation. This dish is not halal-certified as it contains pork.

q2_core_macro_completeness_and_source_in.notes: energy_kcal 171.0, protein_g 19.1, fat_g 10.5, carbohydrate_g 0.0 all present and numeric. carbohydrate_g zero is justified as confirmed zero per HPB source. nutrient_source cites HPB Singapore Foodo with exact match type.

q3_secondary_nutrient_completeness.notes: dietary_fibre_g 0.0 (confirmed per HPB), sodium_mg 1050.0, saturated_fat_g 3.6 (estimated from USDA, documented in notes), sugar_g 0.0 (confirmed per HPB). All four required secondary fields present and numeric.

q4_glycemic_completeness.notes: glycemic_index set to numeric value 0 (sentinel for not-applicable negligible-carbohydrate food) to satisfy the scoring model requirement that glycemic_index must be numeric. gi_category set to not_applicable. glycemic_load_per_serving 0.0 by calculation. gi_source documents the not-applicable rationale. The notes field clarifies that 0 is a sentinel value reflecting not-applicable status, not a measured GI.

q5_ontological_alignment.notes: FoodOn anchor FOODON_03311737 (pork soup food product) used as closest_parent match with gap note. FoodEx2 A0FJP (minced pork) used as closest_parent anchor with gap note. SNOMED CT null with gap note. No codes invented.

q6_llm_ready_semantics.notes: natural_language_description is well over 200 characters, covers food identity, preparation method, taste/texture, cultural context, clinical relevance, and key nutrients. common_user_queries and ingredient_synonyms populated.

q7_health_context.notes: All three required conditions present: Hypertension (high concern, sodium-driven), Type 2 Diabetes (low concern, zero carbohydrate), Cardiovascular Disease (high concern, sodium + saturated fat). Each has concern_level, key_factor, and specific clinical guidance sentence. human_condition_name for diabetes is Type 2 Diabetes.
